items.title: 'Very troubled project': Hospital cost blowout concerns treasurer

children.content: The new Women's and Children's Hospital was priced at $3.2 billion in 2022, before inflation and other pressures sent costs in the construction industry soaring.

articles.title: GST push to follow SA budget, treasurer says

children.content: South Australia's treasurer has foreshadowed a push to secure GST revenue, calling on MPs across the political spectrum to step up their advocacy efforts — but the opposition has questioned why a recent GST windfall is not being used to counter soaring debt.

articles.title: No surprises but risks aplenty in Tom Koutsantonis's comeback budget

children.content: Some things haven't changed since Labor Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis last handed down a state budget in 2017.

articles.title: Are you a winner or loser in this year's SA budget?

children.content: South Australian Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis has unveiled the 2026-27 state budget. Here are the winners and losers.

items.title: Future of iconic wine Wolf Blass unclear amid large brand overhaul

children.content: A cloud hangs over the future of the famous Barossa brand as its maker, Treasury Wine Estates, looks to cull more than half of its labels.

items.title: Public servant claims threat of $4m damages bill in golf course fight

children.content: A South Australian public servant taking the state government to court over a controversial redevelopment in the Adelaide Parklands says he will continue to fight the case despite it potentially leading to him losing his house or becoming bankrupt.

items.title: Lorna has lived through two world wars, two pandemics and 26 Australian prime ministers

children.content: Lorna Henstridge will mark her 112th birthday today surrounded by family in South Australia.

items.title: Body-in-suitcase killer loses bid to reduce sentence for double murder

children.content: The NSW Court of Criminal Appeal dismisses a bid by Daniel Holdom to have his sentence reduced after he pleaded guilty in 2018 to murdering his girlfriend and her two-year-old daughter before dumping the child's body in a suitcase on the side of a remote highway.

image.altText: Khandalyce Kiara Pearce and Karlie Jade Pearce-Stevenson

items.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash

children.content: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

items.title: Honouring the man who kept cinema alive in remote outback city

children.content: The Silver City Cinema in remote NSW has stood the test of time for nearly 60 years. Now its new owners want to honour the legend who kept it alive.

items.title: Paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy leaves prison after 25 years

children.content: Notorious paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy has taken his first steps of freedom after serving a 25-year prison term for the sexual abuse of four children.

items.title: 'Sick and tired': Northern Adelaide hospital nurses walk off job for 24 hours

children.content: Nurses and midwives at the Lyell McEwin Hospital have walked off the job as pay negotiations with the state government continue.

items.title: A US ally is getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS. Here's how

children.content: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

image.altText: Three men in white uniforms and caps salute at the entrance on top of a large grey submarine, with a South Korean flag behind.

items.title: Full Circle launches professional musical theatre company in Adelaide

children.content: Musical theatre typically draws strong audiences in Adelaide where a troupe dedicated to the genre has just launched a professional company that will produce Catch Me If You Can in November.

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

image.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: Heatwaves are becoming so extreme, they're 'breaking the biology' of plants

children.content: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

image.altText: a generic photo of a wheat crop

items.title: 'Too dangerous to push ahead': Aussie diver leaves Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards is on his way back to Australia, after multiple days helping rescue miners trapped in a Laos cave.

items.title: Could a 'mansion tax' help the housing crisis?

children.content: Amid some of the most drastic proposed changes to Australia's tax system in decades and years of skyrocketing home values, one leading expert says it is an idea "worth considering".

articles.title: Home owners told no need for 'panicking' amid Sydney property slump

children.content: Sydney property prices have dipped 0.9 per cent in the past month, following a tepid six months. But how worried should home owners be?

items.title: Fastest-rising women's cancer in Australia still has a low profile

children.content: The incidence of the cancer has doubled over the past 25 years. But it's also a disease without a public profile, which means many women aren't aware of the warning signs or the risk factors.

items.title: Acid attack victim's lawyer describes trial of perpetrators as a 'sham'

children.content: Four military officers are facing trial over an acid attack on an Indonesian human rights campaigner that left him seriously disfigured. But some lawyers say the legal process is a "sham".

items.title: The 20-minute coffee van that keeps a small community together

children.content: Residents of the tiny town of Underbool, in Victoria's remote Mallee region, flock to this small coffee van, which has even smaller opening hours.

items.title: Putin says he sees 'no point' meeting Zelenskyy

children.content: A Ukrainian naval drone has exploded in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta, as European leaders warned the war in Ukraine is becoming a "direct threat" to countries along the EU's eastern border.

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

items.title: How Matt Corby overcame 19 years of fear and found magic in tragedy

children.content: He got his start as a singing TV idol, but Matt Corby is making music for himself now, finding the magic in grief, growth and family.
